message:
feat(rch/fleet): per-fleet cooperative lock for deploy/rollback/drain (bd-5z2wa)

Two concurrent `rch fleet {deploy,rollback,drain}` invocations from
different NTM agent panes (or any other scheduler) could race on
worker state — systemd unit writes, binary swaps, drain/undrain
sequencing — with no synchronization guard at the CLI layer. The
symptom surfaced as "Worker self-test failed" in 30+ sessions across
asupersync subagent runs where two panes were deploying concurrently
within minutes of each other (evidence: css sessions 9fa44cdd,
bef2127d, 455a2294).

This change introduces a process-level cooperative lock for any
state-mutating fleet operation:

  * New `rch::fleet::lock` module (rch/src/fleet/lock.rs):
    - Lock file: `$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/rch/fleet_op.lock`, falling back to
      `${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch-<uid>/fleet_op.lock`.
    - Acquisition is an atomic `O_CREAT|O_EXCL` create; the file
      contents are a single line `<pid> <operation> <start-unix-seconds>`
      so a contending caller can report who holds the lock.
    - Release via RAII: `FleetLockGuard::drop` removes the file
      (best-effort, logs a warning on failure but never panics).
    - Stale lock recovery: if the pid recorded in the file is no
      longer running (`/proc/<pid>` check on Linux), the file is
      removed and acquisition retried once.
    - Optional waiting: `RCH_FLEET_WAIT_SECS=<n>` in the environment
      makes a contending caller poll for the holder to release;
      default is fail-fast so automation (including repeated NTM pane
      marches) gets a clear signal rather than silently queueing.

  * Wire-up in `rch::fleet::{deploy, rollback, drain}`
    (rch/src/fleet/mod.rs):
    - Dry-run paths are read-only and skip the lock, so a dry-run
      probe can be issued while a real deploy is in flight.
    - Real deploys/rollbacks/drains acquire the lock via
      `lock::acquire("fleet-{op}")` after dry-run handling and keep
      it for the rest of the function; the guard lives at function
      scope and is dropped automatically on any exit path.
    - Lock-acquisition failure surfaces through the normal JSON
      error envelope (`ErrorCode::InternalStateError`) in JSON mode
      and a styled error line in human mode, then returns Ok(())
      without partial side effects.

This complements the wrapper guard in `scripts/multi_agent_safety.sh
rch fleet deploy ...` which uses `flock` at the host level. The
in-process guard protects callers who don't know to use the wrapper.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
